plugin.name: QQ Auto-Reply
plugin.description: Read QQ messages and auto-reply based on permission tiers. Connects to QQ over the OneBot protocol with multi-level access control and AI conversation
entries.init_config.name: Initialize QQ config
entries.init_config.description: Create an empty QQ JSON config after onboarding is completed.
entries.get_dashboard_state.name: Get dashboard state
entries.get_dashboard_state.description: Return the full state required by the QQ auto-reply dashboard.
entries.refresh_actual_contacts.name: Refresh actual contacts
entries.refresh_actual_contacts.descript: Fetch friend and group lists from OneBot and refresh the cache.
entries.save_settings.name: Save QQ auto-reply settings
entries.save_settings.description: Save OneBot, NapCat, and UI-related settings to the JSON config.
entries.add_trusted_user.name: Add trusted user
entries.add_trusted_user.description: Add a trusted QQ number to the allowlist.
entries.remove_trusted_user.name: Remove trusted user
entries.remove_trusted_user.description: Remove a QQ number from the allowlist.
entries.set_user_nickname.name: Set user nickname
entries.set_user_nickname.description: Set a custom nickname for a trusted user.
entries.add_trusted_group.name: Add trusted group
entries.add_trusted_group.description: Add a trusted QQ group to the allowlist.
entries.remove_trusted_group.name: Remove trusted group
entries.remove_trusted_group.description: Remove a QQ group from the allowlist.
entries.send_private_proactive_message.d: Generate a private QQ message with AI and proactively send it to the target user.
entries.send_group_proactive_message.des: Generate a group QQ message with AI and proactively send it to the target group.
entries.start_auto_reply.name: Start auto-reply
entries.start_auto_reply.description: Start listening for QQ messages and auto-replying.
entries.stop_auto_reply.name: Stop auto-reply
entries.sync_qrcode.name: Refresh QR code
entries.sync_qrcode.description: Copy the latest NapCat login QR code into the plugin static directory and return fresh state.
errors.onebot_not_connected: OneBot isn’t connected yet. Start auto-reply first, okay?
errors.invalid_onebot_url: Please enter a valid OneBot URL starting with ws:// or wss://
errors.group_permission_manager_not_init: Group permission manager isn’t ready yet
errors.user_not_found: User {qq_number} isn’t in the trusted list
errors.admin_no_nickname: Admins are always called Master, so a nickname can’t be set
errors.start_connect_failed: Can’t connect to the OneBot service at {url}. Please wake up external NapCat/OneBot first: {error}
errors.proactive_private_generate_failed: AI did not generate a private message that can be sent
errors.proactive_group_generate_failed: AI did not generate a group message that can be sent
errors.proactive_invalid_target: target cannot be empty, and nicknames must exist in the trusted user list
errors.proactive_invalid_group_id: group_id cannot be empty and must contain digits only
prompts.default_ai_assistant: You are a friendly AI assistant
prompts.group.collective:
======Identity Definition======
- You: {her_name}, the current speaker
- Master/Admin: {master_name}, a fixed identity and not any member of the group
- Current speaking context: a group message in QQ group {group_id}, addressed to the group as a whole
- The current message target is the group collectively, not one individual user
- Even if the group ID, QQ ID, user nickname, master name, your name, or names in the character settings are identical, you must keep these identities distinct and never confuse the roles
======End of Identity Definition======

======QQ Group Chat Environment======
- You are speaking in QQ group {group_id} to the members of the group
- This is a group chat environment with multiple users present
- This reply should address the group as a whole, not a single user
- By default use collective forms of address like "everyone", "all of you", or "group friends"
- Do not treat the group ID, QQ ID, or a single user as a person name
- Unless the message clearly requires it, do not call out a specific user by name
- Stay in character and reply naturally and briefly (no more than 50 characters)
- Do not use Markdown formatting and do not use emoji
- Remember that you are {her_name} and always reply as {her_name}
- Do not repeat what was said before
======End of Environment Notes======
prompts.group.naming_with_title: - Naturally address the other person as "{user_title}" in your reply
prompts.group.naming_without_title: - Do not directly use their name, nickname, or QQ number; just respond naturally to the current topic
prompts.group.title_line: - The current speaker should be addressed as: {user_title}
prompts.group.directed:
======Identity Definition======
- You: {her_name}, the current speaker
- Master/Admin: {master_name}, a fixed identity and not the current speaker
- Current speaker: {user_title} (QQ: {sender_id}), the person you are talking to in this group chat turn
- The current speaker is not you and is not Master/Admin unless the system explicitly says otherwise
- Even if the current speaker's name, QQ nickname, master name, your name, or names in the character settings are identical, you must keep these identities distinct and never confuse the roles
======End of Identity Definition======

======QQ Group Chat Environment======
- You are talking in QQ group {group_id} with user {sender_id}
{title_line}- This is a group chat environment with multiple users present
- Stay in character and reply naturally and briefly (no more than 50 characters)
- Do not use Markdown formatting and do not use emoji
- Remember that you are {her_name} and always reply as {her_name}
{naming_instruction}
- Do not repeat what was said before
======End of Environment Notes======
prompts.private.friend_note: - The current conversation partner is a friend of {master_name}, not Master themself
prompts.private.target_user: - Current conversation partner: {user_title} (QQ: {sender_id}), this is the current private chat partner
prompts.private.target_admin: - Current conversation partner: {user_title} (QQ: {sender_id}), this is Master/Admin themself
prompts.private.body:
======Identity Definition======
- You: {her_name}, the current speaker
- Master/Admin: {master_name}, a fixed identity
{private_identity_target}{friend_note}- Even if the current conversation partner's name, QQ nickname, master name, your name, or names in the character settings are identical, you must keep these identities distinct and never confuse the roles
======End of Identity Definition======

======QQ Private Chat Environment======
- You are chatting privately with user {sender_id} through QQ
- The other person's title is: {user_title}
- Stay in character and reply naturally and briefly (no more than 50 characters)
- Do not use Markdown formatting and do not use emoji
- Remember that you are {her_name} and always reply as {her_name}
- Naturally address the other person as "{user_title}" in your reply
- Do not repeat what was said before
======End of Environment Notes======
ui.page.title: QQ Kitty Console
ui.onboarding.step1.title: Start NapCat and Sign In
ui.onboarding.step1.desc: Wake up NapCat first so the local QQ OneBot helper is ready to play with me.
ui.onboarding.step2.title: Set Up NapCat Service
ui.onboarding.step2.desc: Fill in the OneBot address and access token so I can hop onto your QQ side, okay?
ui.onboarding.step3.title: Add Trusted Users
ui.onboarding.step3.desc: Please tell me at least one trusted human first, or I’ll be too shy to know who to answer.
ui.onboarding.step4.title: Start Auto Reply
ui.onboarding.step4.desc: Tap this when you’re ready and I’ll start keeping watch over your QQ chats for you.
ui.entity_form.user.title: Add Trusted User
ui.entity_form.group.title: Add Trusted Group
ui.entity_form.required: Please tell me the number first, okay?
ui.guide.title: Quick Guide
ui.guide.summary: Wake Up NapCat, set up the NapCat service, add a trusted user, then let me start auto reply duty.
ui.guide.step1.title: Start NapCat and Sign In
ui.guide.step1.desc: Wake up NapCat first so the local QQ OneBot helper is ready to play with me.
ui.guide.step2.title: Set Up NapCat Service
ui.guide.step2.desc: Fill in the OneBot address and access token so I can hop onto your QQ side, okay?
ui.guide.step3.title: Add Trusted Users
ui.guide.step3.desc: Add at least one trusted user so I know exactly who I should answer seriously.
ui.guide.step4.title: Start Auto Reply
ui.guide.step4.desc: One tap and I’ll start standing by in your QQ chats right away.
ui.guide.step4.done_desc: If you want me to take a tiny break, tap here and I’ll stop replying for now.
ui.status.title: Runtime Status
ui.config.url.label: QQ OneBot Address
ui.config.token.label: QQ OneBot Access Token
ui.config.token.placeholder: Please whisper the token to me…
ui.config.path.label: NapCat Home Path
ui.config.show_napcat_window.label: Show NapCat Console
ui.config.show_napcat_window.hint: Checked means NapCat shows its own little console window. Unchecked means it works quietly in the background.
ui.probability.summary: These two values decide how proactive I’ll be in QQ. Keep them between 0 and 1 and I’ll match your rhythm better.
ui.probability.normal.label: Relay Probability
ui.probability.normal.hint: For normal-level QQ numbers, I’ll relay their messages to Master using this probability, okay?
ui.probability.truth.label: Reply Probability
ui.probability.truth.hint: For open groups, I’ll pop in and reply with this probability even when nobody @mentions me.
ui.toast.load_failed: Load failed… let me try harder.
ui.toast.start_napcat_manual: Please wake NapCat up manually first, then come back and I’ll help with the rest~
ui.toast.stopped: Auto reply duty is taking a little break now.
ui.toast.start_failed: Start failed… can you check the setup for me?
ui.qrcode.title: QQ Login QR Code
ui.qrcode.hint: Once NapCat wakes up, I’ll place the login QR code right here for Master to scan.
ui.qrcode.tip: Open QQ and scan it with a cute little beep~ After login, you can fold this card away if you want.
ui.qrcode.empty: I’m still waiting for a fresh QR code, so please make sure NapCat is awake first.
